edge_hypothesis: The MACD 7:1 tail harvester is the single best strategy in the system (PF=1.712, DD=7.5%, 161 trades, all regimes profitable). It uses a 7-bar time cap and 1.0x ATR stop. The existing Claude specs (mc6t8b3k, mc4w9s2t) test LONGER time caps (6/8/10 bars) and WIDER stops (1.5x ATR). Nobody has tested the OPPOSITE direction: SHORTER time caps (3-4 bars) with the champion's tight stop. The untested thesis: the MACD histogram zero-line cross is a LEADING signal — when the histogram flips from negative to positive (with close > EMA_50 confirmation), the resulting move either happens IMMEDIATELY (within 3-4 bars) or the signal is stale. On 1h, a 4-bar cap = 4 hours. If the MACD cross hasn't produced a directional move in 4 hours, the histogram has likely flattened or reversed. By exiting stale signals faster, we avoid the slow bleed of bars 5-7 where the champion's 7-bar cap still holds losing positions. At 9:1 R:R (1.5x stop, 13.5x TP), the reward is higher than the champion's 7:1 — each winner compensates for more losers. The lower win rate from the aggressive time cap should be offset by the larger per-winner payoff. There's also a 3-bar ultra-aggressive variant with the champion's original 1.0x ATR stop at 8:1 R:R — testing whether the champion's edge is ENTIRELY concentrated in bars 1-3. Invalidation: if PF < champion's 1.712, the 7-bar cap is optimal and the histogram cross signal needs more time to develop than 3-4 bars.

variants.name: macd_1h_immediacy_9to1_cap4
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross with 9:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop, 13.5x ATR TP) and aggressive 4-bar time cap on ETH/1h. Tests the 'immediacy hypothesis': MACD crosses that produce profitable moves do so within 4 bars. After 4 bars, the signal decays. The 1.5x ATR stop (wider than champion's 1.0x) gives slightly more room for the trade to develop, but the 4-bar cap is more aggressive than any existing MACD variant. At 9:1 R:R, each winner = 9 losses. Even at 15% win rate, PF > 1.3. Expected: fewer trades than champion (some get time-capped at bar 4-5 that the champion would hold to bar 7), but the trades that DO win are larger. If the champion's average winner develops in bars 1-4, this variant should have similar PF with lower DD.
entry_long: MACD histogram (MACDh_12_26_9) crosses above zero (momentum shift to bullish).
Close > EMA_50 (trend confirmation — price above medium-term average).
entry_short: MACD histogram (MACDh_12_26_9) crosses below zero (momentum shift to bearish).
Close < EMA_50 (trend confirmation — price below medium-term average).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (13.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Exit at market after 4 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (immediacy filter — 4 hours on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.3, the MACD histogram cross needs more than 4 bars to develop — the 7-bar champion cap is closer to optimal
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 13.5x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 4 bars at market price.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend confirmation — close > EMA_50 for longs
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-line cross — primary signal, momentum shift detection
ATR_14 for 1.5x stop / 13.5x TP — 9:1 R:R with 4-bar immediacy cap

variants.name: macd_1h_ultra_tight_8to1_cap3
variants.description: Ultra-aggressive MACD variant: champion's 1.0x ATR stop with 8:1 R:R (1.0x stop, 8.0x TP) and 3-bar time cap. This is the most extreme time discipline in the entire system. 3 bars on 1h = 3 hours. The hypothesis: the MACD champion's best trades are immediate — the histogram crosses zero, price explodes in the first 2-3 bars, and the 1.0x ATR tight stop means only strong-conviction moves survive. Bars 4-7 of the champion's time cap may be holding onto already-stale signals. If the champion's PF is driven by bars 1-3, this ultra-tight variant should have HIGHER PF with fewer total trades (the bar 4-7 trades are removed). At 8:1 R:R with the champion's 1.0x stop, this is a direct descendant of the tail harvester approach applied with maximum aggression.
entry_long: MACD histogram (MACDh_12_26_9) crosses above zero (momentum shift to bullish).
entry_short: MACD histogram (MACDh_12_26_9) crosses below zero (momentum shift to bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (8.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
Exit at market after 3 bars if neither TP nor SL has been hit (ultra-aggressive time cap — 3 hours on 1h).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < champion's 1.712, the 7-bar cap captures profitable trades in bars 4-7 that this variant misses — the MACD signal is not as immediately explosive as theorized
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 8.0x ATR_14 from entry.
Time exit after 3 bars at market price.

components.notes: MACDh_12_26_9 zero-line cross — champion's signal with ultra-tight time discipline
ATR_14 for 1.0x stop / 8.0x TP — 8:1 R:R with 3-bar ultra-aggressive time cap (champion's stop level)
